neutral.summary: Electric vehicle sales jumped 45% in the last quarter, driven by new models and expanded charging infrastructure across major cities.

left.take: Good progress, but we need stronger emissions standards and more public transit investment. Can't rely on individual car purchases to save the planet.

quotes.text: EVs are part of the solution, but public transportation is the real answer to climate change.
quotes.speaker: Climate Activist Jamie Lee

quotes.text: We're just replacing one kind of car dependency with another. We need trains, not Teslas.
quotes.speaker: Urban Transit Advocate Maria Santos

quotes.text: Electric cars still require massive mining operations that devastate communities. This isn't sustainability.
quotes.speaker: Environmental Justice Organizer Kwame Jones

quotes.text: The charging infrastructure is being built in wealthy neighborhoods while working-class areas are left behind.
quotes.speaker: Sen. Amanda Foster

right.take: The market is working exactly as it should. Innovation and consumer choice - not government mandates - are driving the transition.

quotes.text: American innovation proves that free markets solve problems better than bureaucrats.
quotes.speaker: Auto Industry Analyst Mike Stevens

quotes.text: Let people drive what they want. The market will figure it out without government interference.
quotes.speaker: Rep. Dan Wilson

quotes.text: These companies succeeded by building products people actually want, not by chasing subsidies.
quotes.speaker: Business Columnist Sarah Johnson

quotes.text: Competition is driving down prices and improving technology. That's how capitalism works.
quotes.speaker: Economic Policy Institute Director Tom Richards

quotes.text: EVs are great, but mandating them is government overreach. Let consumers choose.
quotes.speaker: Gov. Patricia Reynolds
